children.text: The adherence of NASA to program cost and schedule targets and discipline across NASA programs remains a concern.
The James Webb Space Telescope has exceeded its cost estimate.
In 2007 the Government Accountability Office issued a report on NASA’s high risk acquisition performance.
In response, NASA prepared a corrective action plan two years ago.

children.heading: Reports required
children.label: 1 Reports required
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , and not later than April 30 of each year thereafter, the Administrator shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report on the implementation during the preceding year for the corrective action plan referred to in subsection (a)(4).October 11, 2010

children.label: A
children.text: A description of each NASA program that has exceeded its cost baseline by 15 percent or more or is more than 2 years behind its projected development schedule.

children.label: B
children.text: For each program specified under subparagraph (A), a plan for such decrease in scope or requirements, or other measures, to be undertaken to control cost and schedule, including any cost monitoring or corrective actions undertaken pursuant to the National Aeronautics and Space Administration Authorization Act of 2005 (), and the amendments made by that Act.Public Law 109–1551See References in Text note below.1
